title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8597, ENTITLED âAN ACT GRANTING THE TRENTO TELEPHONE SYSTEM, INCORPORATED A FRANCHISE TO CONSTRUCT, ESTABLISH, INSTALL, MAINTAIN AND OPERATE LOCAL EXCHANGE NETWORK IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF TRENTO, PROVINCE OF AGUSAN DEL SURâ

chunks.text:
H. No. 5882 / 100 OG No. 48, 5882 (Nov. 29, 2004) ; Tribune 10/5/04 ; Malaya 10/ 5/04

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9320, August 08, 2004 ]

AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8597, ENTITLED âAN ACT GRANTING THE TRENTO TELEPHONE SYSTEM, INCORPORATED A FRANCHISE TO CONSTRUCT, ESTABLISH, INSTALL, MAINTAIN AND OPERATE LOCAL EXCHANGE NETWORK IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF TRENTO, PROVINCE OF AGUSAN DEL SURâ

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. Section 1 of Republic Act No. 8597 is hereby amended to read as follows:

âSECTION 1.

Nature and Scope of Franchise.

â Subject to the provisions of the Constitution and applicable laws, rules and regulations, there is hereby granted to the Trento Telephone System, Incorporated, hereunder referred to as the grantee, its successors or assigns, a franchise to construct, establish, install, maintain and operate for commercial purposes and in the public interest, local exchange network, including public calling stations or pay telephone stations or wireless local loop and for such purpose provide basic telephone service or other means related to the foregoing now known to science or which in the future may be developed in the municipalities of Trento, Bunawan, Sta. Josefa, Rosario, Veruela and Loreto, all in the Province of Agusan del Sur, for public domestic telecommunications.â

SEC. 2. The title of Republic Act No. 8597 is hereby amended to read as follows:

âAn Act Granting the Trento Telephone System, Incorporated a Franchise to Construct, Establish, Install, Maintain and Operate Local Exchange Network in the Municipalities of Trento, Bunawan, Sta. Josefa, Rosario, Veruela and Loreto, All in the Province of Agusan del Surâ.

SEC. 3.

Effectivity Clause

. â This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days from the date of its publication, upon the initiative of the grantee, in at least two (2) newspapers of general circulation in the Philippines.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JOSE DE VENECIA JR.

(Sgd.) FRANKLIN M. DRILON

Speaker of the House of Representatives

President of the Senate

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on May 26, 2003 and June 10, 2004, respectively.

(Sgd.) ROBERTO P. NAZARENO

(Sgd.) OSCAR G. YABES

Secretary General

House of Representatives

Secretary of the Senate

Approved:

GLORIA MACAPAGAL-ARROYO

President of the Philippines

* Lapsed into law on August 8, 2004, without the signature of the President, in accordance with Article VI Section 27 (1) of the Constitution.
